svg.title: MN 16 Cetokhila Sutta — Barriers of the Mind
svg.desc: How growth in Dhamma and Vinaya becomes possible: five barriers of the mind presented as thought-bubbles and cleared by confidence; five shackles presented and severed; fifteen factors; and the hen-and-egg simile of inevitable breakthrough.

g.text: “How does one achieve growth, increase, and full development in this Dhamma and Vinaya?”
FIVE BARRIERS OF THE MIND · PAÑCA CETOKHILĀ
Mind does not incline toward effort, examination, perseverance, striving
cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya
Mind inclines — confidence clears the barriers
cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya
FIVE SHACKLES OF THE MIND · PAÑCA CETASOVINIBANDHĀ
Mind does not incline toward effort, examination, perseverance, striving
cittaṁ na namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya
Mind inclines — freedom clears the shackles
cittaṁ namati ātappāya anuyogāya sātaccāya padhānāya
When barriers are abandoned and shackles severed,
growth in this teaching and training is possible
pañca cetokhilā pahīnā · pañca cetasovinibandhā susamucchinnā · ṭhānametaṁ vijjati
CULTIVATES THE FOUR BASES OF PSYCHIC POWERS · CATTĀRO IDDHIPĀDE BHĀVETI
Endowed with these fifteen factors including active involvement
“Even if the wish does not arise in her … those chicks are capable of breaking through.”
Capable of breaking through · capable of full awakening · unsurpassed security from bondage
bhabbo abhinibbidāya · bhabbo sambodhāya · bhabbo anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya
“So too, one endowed with these fifteen factors is capable of breaking through,
of full awakening, and of attaining the unsurpassed security from bondage.”
